what_it_does: This is the pnpm lockfile (lockfileVersion 9.0) for the root workspace importer of canvas/app; it records the exact resolved versions, integrity hashes, peer-dependency metadata, and transitive package graph so that installs are reproducible.
observations: lockfileVersion is '9.0' with autoInstallPeers: true and excludeLinksFromLockfile: false
root importer declares dependencies react@18.3.1, react-dom@18.3.1, and tldraw@3.15.6
root devDependencies are @vitejs/plugin-react@4.7.0, typescript@5.9.3, and vite@6.4.2
packages section contains many transitive dependencies including Babel 7.29.7, esbuild 0.25.12, Floating UI, Radix UI primitives, Tiptap editor extensions, and Rollup 4.60.4
resolved versions often exceed the caret specifiers, e.g. tldraw ^3.13.1 resolved to 3.15.6 and typescript ^5.6.3 resolved to 5.9.3
the file is truncated mid-way through the '@tiptap/extension-paragraph' entry
standards: tldraw as a direct dependency signals a canvas/whiteboard application
Vite 6 + @vitejs/plugin-react + React 18 form the build/runtime foundation
a large transitive UI stack is pulled in via Radix UI and Tiptap editor extensions
conventions: follows the pnpm v9 lockfile schema with separate 'importers' and 'packages' sections
uses caret semver ranges in specifiers while pinning exact resolved versions and SRI integrity hashes
concerns: the file content is truncated, so the lockfile is incomplete in this view
resolved versions are higher than the specifiers, which is normal for caret ranges but worth confirming
notes: This is the pinned dependency graph for a Vite+React canvas app centered on tldraw, with a sizable Radix/Tiptap UI and editor transitive tree.
questions: Which app features consume tldraw versus the Tiptap editor extensions?
Does the truncated remainder contain additional workspace importers or pnpm overrides?
Why did TypeScript resolve to 5.9.3 when the specifier is ^5.6.3?
Are the optional @types/react peer dependencies actually needed or satisfied?
purpose_vs_actual: matches its purpose: it is a pnpm lockfile that pins exact resolved versions and integrity hashes for the declared dependency tree.
intention_signals: It appears to lock down a deterministic install for a React+Vite application that combines a tldraw canvas with a Radix UI and Tiptap-based editor/interface layer.
novelty: The combination of tldraw and Tiptap editor extensions in one direct dependency set suggests a hybrid whiteboard-plus-rich-text-editor application.
contribution: It contributes the exact resolved dependency graph and integrity hashes that a fused system would keep to preserve reproducible installs and transitive package identities for this workspace.
summary_for_embedding: pnpm v9 lockfile for the root importer of company/canvas/app, pinning React 18.3.1, tldraw 3.15.6, Vite 6.4.2, TypeScript 5.9.3, and their transitive dependencies including Radix UI, Tiptap, esbuild, and Rollup.
intention_for_embedding: Capture a reproducible, exact dependency resolution for a Vite-based React canvas application using tldraw and a Radix/Tiptap UI stack.
